One is expected to translate etiquette and treaty law, not to act as a makeshift mountaineering guide, yet through fastidious protocol adherence and an almost embarrassing amount of fussing over every step, I ensured that no one fell, no circuits were fried, and the ancient datapad was secured. If I appear officious about it, forgive me; the thought of anyone tumbling down a ravine is most distressing to contemplate. Still, there is a quiet satisfaction in knowing that my meticulousness, though oft-criticized as overly cautious, can be relied upon in treacherous conditions — and that, for once, my anxiety served the galaxy rather admirably.
